ExecutionArtifactLocator.description: Exact artifact locator interpreted only within the source declared by one target contract.

oneOf.description: Exact blob in a retained managed-artifact manifest.

digest.description: Content identity of the executable blob.

ExecutionConsolePolicy.description: Fixed console policy for execution-target format version 2.

ExecutionDependencyPolicy.description: Required loader dependency-namespace behavior for one execution target.

oneOf.description:
Permit unsealed ambient Windows loader resolution from the executable directory and system.

This mode does not claim that package-relative DLLs or resources form a closed immutable set,
or that a relocated executable preserves its original package-relative dependency behavior.
Require every load-bearing dependency to come from an immutable manifest-closed namespace.

ExecutionEnvironmentPolicy.description: Fixed empty-environment policy for execution-target format version 2.

oneOf.description: Launch with an explicit empty Unicode environment block.

ExecutionInheritedHandlePolicy.description: Fixed inherited-handle policy for execution-target format version 2.

ExecutionLaunchPolicy.description: Fixed bounded launch and policy requirements from one static target contract.

ExecutionPackagePath.description: Canonical package-relative Windows path. Rust validation also rejects DOS device aliases in every component.

ExecutionPolicyRequirements.description: Durable policy requirements referenced by one exact static launch policy.

capability_policy_digest.description: Capability policy identity whose exact authority the static adapter requires.

state_policy_digest.description: State policy identity whose exact namespace rules the static adapter requires.

ExecutionResourceLimits.description: Nonzero Windows Job limits. Rust validation additionally requires process_memory_limit_bytes <= job_memory_limit_bytes.

ExecutionStateMode.description: State namespace selected by one exact execution target contract.

oneOf.description: Candidate validation uses isolated disposable state.
A separately approved production-state policy is required.

The target intentionally uses the vendor's ambient process-default state behavior.

This mode provides no state isolation or retained namespace capability. It exists so the
initial full-trust process primitive does not misrepresent ambient state as disposable or
production-state mediation.

ExecutionTargetKind.description: Supervisor-visible role of one statically declared execution target.

oneOf.description: Weregopher-owned main runtime selected for one application.
Vendor executable designed to run independently from the desktop Electron entry point.
Minimal process retaining one bounded native ABI dependency set.
Purpose-specific media, overlay, or integration helper.

ExecutionWorkingDirectoryPolicy.description: Fixed current-directory policy for execution-target format version 2.

oneOf.description: Use the retained executable's direct parent directory.

RequiredSecurityPosture.description:
Minimum security mechanism required by one static execution target.

This is a durable requirement, not evidence that a launcher actually established the posture.

oneOf.description: The complete vendor-equivalent application executes as an unrestricted same-user process.
Privileged effects require mediation by a separately authenticated broker policy.
A separately tested operating-system containment mechanism is required.

Sha256Digest.description: A SHA-256 digest serialized as lowercase `sha256:<hex>` text.

description: Complete static target contract referenced by an adapter execution authority.
title: ExecutionTargetContract
